title: Customizing Scales, Labels, and Themes
description: Add titles, axis labels, change colors, and apply themes.

slides.title: Adding Labels with labs()
slides.content:
Use `labs()` to add titles, axis labels, and captions.

```r
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Scatterplot of Weight vs MPG",
       x = "Weight (1000 lbs)",
       y = "Miles per Gallon",
       caption = "Source: mtcars")
```

slides.title: Add Labels
slides.codeSnippet:
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "MPG vs Weight by Cylinder",
       x = "Weight (1000 lbs)",
       y = "MPG",
       color = "Cylinders")

quizData.question: Which function adds a title to a ggplot?

slides.title: Customizing Scales
slides.content:
Scales control how aesthetics are displayed. Examples:

- `scale_x_continuous()` – customize x-axis
- `scale_y_log10()` – log transform y
- `scale_color_manual()` – set specific colors

```r
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("red", "blue", "green"))
```

slides.title: Manual Colors
slides.codeSnippet:
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73"))

slides.title: Themes
slides.content:
Themes control non-data elements like background, grid lines, font. Several built-in themes:
- `theme_bw()` – white background
- `theme_minimal()` – minimal
- `theme_classic()` – classic look
- `theme_dark()` – dark

```r
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +
  geom_point() +
  theme_bw()
```

slides.title: Apply a Theme
slides.codeSnippet:
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg)) +
  geom_point() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Minimal Theme")
